content.full_text:
August 15, 1990


Lois M. Shade
811 E. Juanita Avenue
Glendora, CA 91740

Re:  Your Request for Confirmation of
     Telephone Advice
     Our File No. A-90-449

Dear Ms. Shade:

     This is to confirm telephone advice provided to you
concerning your obligations regarding surplus campaign funds under
the Political Reform Act (the "Act).¹

QUESTIONS²

     1.   May you retain your surplus campaign funds for your use
in a future campaign?

     2.   May the surplus campaign funds be used to publish a
newsletter providing only general information to the Glendora com-
munity?

     3.   What is the last date for disposing of surplus campaign
funds?

     4.   May the surplus campaign funds be retained until the
organization to which you wish to contribute achieves the
requisite tax-exempt, charitable status?

CONCLUSION

     1&2.  Once campaign funds becomes surplus funds, the Act limits
their use.  Permissible use of surplus funds raised after January

_______________
¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.  Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq.  All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

² These questions are formulated based upon our two telephone
conversations of June 28, 1990, and your letter of the same date.


428 J Street, Suite 800 ● P.O. Box 807 ● Sacramento CA 95804-0807 ● (916)322-5660

Our File No. A-90-449
Page 2

1, 1990, does not include funding your future campaign or
publishing a newsletter.

3. Surplus funds need not be disposed of by any date
certain.

4. Surplus funds may be retained until the intended
recipient organization achieves tax-exempt status so long as
campaign statements are regularly filed.

FACTS

You presented the following facts in our two telephone
conversations of June 28, 1990. You are an unsuccessful candidate
for city council. The election was held in April 1990. You have
funds remaining in your campaign account.

ANALYSIS

Campaign funds under the control of a candidate become
surplus funds at the end of the postelection period following the defeat of the candidate. (Section 85807.)³ In your
case the postelection period ended on June 30, 1990. (Section
84200.) Once campaign funds become surplus, their use is limited.
Under Section 85807, surplus campaign funds can be used only for
the following purposes:

(a) The payment of outstanding campaign
debts or elected officer's expenses.

(b) The pro rata repayment of
contributions.

(c) Donations to any bona fide
charitable, educational, civic, religious, or
similar tax-exempt, nonprofit organization,
where no substantial part of the proceeds will
have a material financial effect on the former
candidate or elected officer, any member of
his or her immediate family, or his or her
campaign treasurer.

(d) Contributions to a political party
or committee so long as the funds are not used
to make contributions in support of or
opposition to a candidate for elective office.

(e) Contributions to support or oppose
any candidate for federal office, any

_____________
3 The Commission staff intends to present a regulation to the
Commission in the near future that will clarify the provisions of
Section 85807.

Our File No. A-90-449
Page 3

     candidate for elective office in a state other
than California, or any ballot measure.

     No other use is permitted.  Thus you may not retain the funds
for your future campaign or to publish a newsletter.

     The Act does not specify a date certain that you must
dispose of the surplus campaign funds.  However, so long as
the campaign account remains active, you must abide by the
filing requirements of the Act.  (See Sections 84200-84226.)
Thus you may retain your surplus campaign funds until the
organization to which you wish to contribute achieves the
requisite tax-exempt, nonprofit status.

     I trust that this letter has provided you with the
guidance requested.  If you have any further questions
regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5901.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Scott Hallabrin
                                        Acting General Counsel

                                        [signature]
                              By:  Mark Morodomi
                                   Counsel, Legal Division
